concerns.text: T011 is marked as unchecked (incomplete) and is the sole task responsible for saving the cleaned dataset to data/processed/cleaned_issues.csv and validating the ≥95% completeness threshold (SC-001). Without this task, the data pipeline output is orphaned, and the spec's SC-001 acceptance criterion is unverified.

T016 is unchecked and is the only task assigned to fit log-normal and Weibull models and report KS/AIC metrics. T015 only generates ECDF plots. Without T016, the spec's US-2 acceptance scenario 2 (fit quality metrics for both families) and SC-002 are unimplemented.

T017 is unchecked and is the sole task for detecting/reporting extreme outliers (>30 days) and their percentage. This directly implements US-2 acceptance scenario 3. Its absence leaves a critical spec requirement unaddressed.

T023 is unchecked and is the only task implementing leave-one-repository-out cross-validation for MAE/R² metrics. This is required by US-3 acceptance scenario 2 and SC-004. Without it, the model's predictive performance cannot be validated per spec.

T025 claims to perform sensitivity analysis but lacks the explicit requirement to sweep the exact thresholds {0.01, 0.05, 0.1} mandated by FR-007. This is a semantic weakening of the functional requirement, risking partial coverage or arbitrary threshold selection.

T039 implements the Reference-Validator Agent but does not include a task to *execute* this agent on artifacts generated in Phases 3-5 before the research_accepted transition. This violates Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) which requires the agent to run as a blocking gate.

T041 validates 'associational' language in result text, but no task ensures this language appears in figure captions/titles generated by T018. FR-008 requires 'all result text', which includes figure annotations. This is a silent relaxation of the scope.
